definition: Foreign official reserve managers have structurally shifted from net Treasury purchasers (~+$300-500B/year, 2003-2013) to approximately flat, adding an estimated 20-40bp to term premium. This reflects petrodollar recycling decline and geopolitically motivated Chinese holdings reduction.
evidence: Official-sector net Treasury purchases shifted to approximately flat
TIC data and IMF COFER confirm structural shift
open_questions: Magnitude depends on limited academic elasticity estimates
Is the decline truly structural or could geopolitical thaw reverse it?
What is the marginal buyer composition replacing official-sector demand?
